feedback.text: Good Morning, two issues I'd like to throw in: 1. "Stop Destroying Videogames" : I am actually losing access to a game I really liked, "Anthem", because the publisher decided to shut off the servers needed for an online only game. Maybe someone can have a thought about some kind of protection for consumers who somehow don't own the thing they buy? 2. Payment Processing: Maybe there is an angle to stop payment processors from deciding what kind of digital medium is offered on the internet? Payment processing should be considered a (neutral) utility, not a service because the level of dependence of modern life ist akin to energy companies.
